About Brant Professional Services

A New York firm where institutional expertise meets genuine partnership.

Who We Are

Straightforward Financial Guidance, Down to the Bottom Line.

Brant Professional Services is a New York-based financial consulting firm founded in 2021. We provide CFO services, tax and accounting, capital advisory, and operational consulting to small and mid-sized businesses, nonprofits, high-net-worth individuals, and entrepreneurs across a wide range of industries.

We started this firm because too many clients were getting financial and accounting advice buried under corporate jargon, slow processes, and unnecessary complexity. Our goal is to take the opposite approach: transparent, practical guidance that’s easy to understand and delivers simple solutions.

We also believe in looking at things from approaches other firms overlook. Some of the most valuable insights come from asking unconventional questions or considering possibilities that are too often overlooked — the kind that surface real savings, real liability, or real opportunity that might otherwise go unnoticed.

And when you call us, you reach a person — not an automated menu. No hold music, no button-pressing to navigate to the right department. Our clients talk directly to us, every time.

Our Approach

We Work Like an Insider.

When we’re engaged, we get to know the business the way someone on the inside would — not just the numbers, but also the relationships, the pressures, and the decisions that don’t show up on a balance sheet. This perspective serves as the foundation for our recommendations, rather than applying a generic framework.

In practice, that means building budgeting and forecasting models that actually reflect how the business runs, reviewing the numbers before the board does, and catching the tax exposure before it becomes a problem. The goal is to make sure financial decisions are made with the full picture in mind.

Who We Serve

The Right Fit Matters.

Our clients tend to be growth-stage and established businesses, entrepreneurs and founders, high-net-worth individuals with complex tax needs, nonprofit organizations, law firms managing trust accounts, and construction ventures requiring tight financial controls. We’ve worked across a broad range of industries and sectors.

What our clients have in common isn’t their industry or their size — it’s their standards. They hold themselves to high standards, they expect their advisors to think a step ahead, and they want a firm that treats their business with the same care they do.

Integrity Without Exception
Every recommendation, every number, every piece of advice is something we would stake our own reputation on. That standard doesn’t waver based on what’s convenient.
Precision as a Baseline
Accuracy isn’t a differentiator for us — it’s the minimum. What sets our work apart is the analytical depth and strategic context behind every deliverable.
Long-Term Orientation
We’re not interested in short-term transactions. The clients we value most want a firm they can trust year after year — through growth, through challenges, and through whatever comes next.
Absolute Discretion
We handle sensitive financial and personal information as a matter of course. That trust is something we protect unconditionally.
Accountability Over Excuses
When something is our responsibility, we own it completely. Our clients never wonder where things stand or chase us for answers — we stay ahead of it.

Why Brant

What Sets Us Apart

The honest answer: we care about the outcome more than the process.

01
Senior-Level Expertise
Every engagement is handled by experienced practitioners. The person you speak with is the person doing the work.
02
Breadth Across the Firm
From CFO services to tax, construction finance, legal accounting, and funding — we cover the full financial lifecycle under one roof.
03
A Partner, Not a Vendor
We don’t submit deliverables and disappear. We stay involved, reachable, and invested in what happens next.
04
A Demonstrated Track Record
We have helped architect financial infrastructure behind a $25M pre-revenue valuation, facilitated a $4.5M capital raise, and managed multi-million dollar construction ventures through closeout.

Ready to Have a Real Conversation?

Whether you need ongoing financial leadership or support with a specific challenge, we’d welcome the opportunity to learn more about your business.

Financial Consulting & Advisory Services

Fractional CFO, tax preparation, bookkeeping, payroll, funding solutions, and strategic advisory — delivered with precision, discretion, and measurable results.

01
CFO Services
Fractional CFOCapital StrategyFinancial ArchitectureBoard Reporting

Brant delivers outsourced CFO leadership at the executive level — providing the financial strategy, governance, and operational oversight that growth-stage and established companies require. We architect the full financial infrastructure: chart of accounts design, ERP integration, multi-entity consolidation, and management reporting frameworks built to institutional standards. We serve as your company’s senior financial voice — in the boardroom, across banking relationships, and at the negotiating table.

Core Deliverables
✓ Monthly close & management reporting
✓ KPI dashboards & variance analysis
✓ Cash flow forecasting & liquidity management
✓ Capital raise preparation & lender packages
✓ Banking & credit facility management
✓ Board & investor reporting
📌 This Service Is Right for You If
Your company is scaling rapidly, preparing for a capital raise, or navigating a strategic inflection point — and needs executive-level financial leadership without the permanent overhead of a full-time C-suite hire.
02
Capital Solutions
Debt & EquityDeal StructuringCapital SyndicationDue Diligence

Brant serves as a strategic capital advisor — connecting businesses with the right funding at the right terms. We specialize in structuring and facilitating debt and equity transactions, including term loan origination, revolving credit facilities, mezzanine debt, and equity syndication across private and institutional capital markets. Our underwriting background allows us to position your company with precision — anticipating lender objections, stress-testing models, and presenting your business in the most compelling light possible.

Core Deliverables
✓ CIM & investor/lender package preparation
✓ Financial model & projection development
✓ Lender identification & outreach strategy
✓ Term sheet review & covenant negotiation
✓ Debt & equity deal structuring
✓ Capital syndication & closing support
📌 This Service Is Right for You If
Your growth objectives require external capital and you need a sophisticated advisor who can build the financial case and navigate institutional relationships.
03
Tax & Accounting
Multi-State ComplianceGAAP ReportingHNWIsEntity Structuring

Brant manages the full spectrum of tax compliance and financial reporting for individuals and entities across every level of complexity. We handle multi-state and federal filings for high-net-worth individuals, corporations, S-corporations, partnerships, and trusts — maintaining strict GAAP compliance throughout. Our engagements go beyond compliance: we identify structural opportunities, optimize entity architecture, and coordinate across all parties to ensure your tax position is both defensible and advantageous.

Core Deliverables
✓ Federal, state & local tax return preparation
✓ Multi-state nexus analysis & compliance
✓ HNWI & trust tax planning
✓ Entity structure optimization
✓ GAAP-compliant GL management
✓ IRS correspondence & audit support
📌 This Service Is Right for You If
Your tax profile has grown beyond what a generalist can manage — involving multiple entities, states, or asset classes — and you require a firm with the depth to both execute and strategize at the highest level.
04
Strategic Advisory
M&A AdvisoryBusiness ValuationGrowth StrategyExit Planning

Brant serves as a trusted strategic advisor to owners, executives, and boards — providing objective, data-driven guidance on the decisions that define an organization’s trajectory. Our engagements span business valuation, M&A targeting and due diligence, competitive positioning, and enterprise-wide financial planning. We translate complex financial and market data into actionable boardroom-ready strategy aligned with long-term enterprise value creation.

Core Deliverables
✓ Business valuation & enterprise value analysis
✓ M&A target identification & due diligence
✓ Strategic financial planning & scenario modeling
✓ Competitive positioning & market analysis
✓ Exit strategy & succession planning
✓ KPI framework development & benchmarking
📌 This Service Is Right for You If
You are navigating a high-stakes decision — an acquisition, ownership transition, capital event, or enterprise repositioning — and require an experienced strategic partner at the table.
05
Compliance
RegulatoryIndustry-SpecificInternal ControlsPolicy & Procedure

In many industries, compliance isn’t a checkbox — it’s a core operating requirement that determines whether the business can function at all. Brant provides compliance advisory services across both general regulatory frameworks and industry-specific requirements. We help clients in heavily regulated sectors — including cannabis, healthcare, and financial services — build the policies, controls, and reporting infrastructure needed to stay in good standing with regulators and auditors. For businesses across all industries, we strengthen internal controls and operational procedures that protect against fraud, error, and exposure.

Core Deliverables
✓ Industry-specific regulatory compliance frameworks
✓ Internal control design & testing
✓ Policy & procedure documentation
✓ Regulatory reporting & filings
✓ Audit preparation & response support
✓ SOX-style controls for private companies
📌 This Service Is Right for You If
You operate in a regulated industry where compliance failures carry real consequences, or you’ve outgrown informal processes and need formal controls and documentation to support continued growth, audits, or institutional funding.
06
Due Diligence
Buyer & SellerInvestment ReviewRisk AssessmentTransaction Support

Major financial decisions deserve careful, independent review before commitments are made. Brant performs due diligence across the full range of transactions where a client’s money, time, or reputation is on the line — including acquisitions, capital raises, lending decisions, investment opportunities, partnership formations, and major contracts. We dig into the financials, the operations, the obligations, and the assumptions, surfacing the risks, opportunities, and questions that should be answered before the deal closes.

Core Deliverables
✓ Financial statement analysis & quality of earnings
✓ Verification of assets, liabilities & cash flows
✓ Tax exposure & contingent liability review
✓ Operational & key person risk assessment
✓ Contract, lease & obligation review
✓ Due diligence reports & recommendations
📌 This Service Is Right for You If
You’re about to make a significant financial decision — buying or selling a business, taking on debt, entering a partnership, making a major investment — and you want an independent set of expert eyes on it before you sign.
07
Technology & Systems
ERP & Accounting SoftwareAutomationCybersecurityWorkflow Design

Every modern business runs on technology, but most are using only a fraction of what their systems can do — or are operating on infrastructure that’s outdated, fragmented, or vulnerable. Brant helps clients build the financial technology backbone that supports how they actually operate. From selecting and implementing the right ERP or accounting platform, to automating manual workflows, integrating disconnected systems, and reinforcing the cybersecurity protections that financial data demands, we focus on the technology decisions that drive efficiency, accuracy, and control.

Core Deliverables
✓ ERP & accounting platform selection
✓ System implementation & integration
✓ Workflow automation & AP/AR streamlining
✓ Cybersecurity assessment & controls
✓ Data migration & reporting infrastructure
✓ Vendor evaluation & implementation oversight
📌 This Service Is Right for You If
Your business is operating on outdated systems, duplicating manual work across disconnected tools, or making major decisions about new technology investments — and you want someone with both the financial expertise and the technical experience to guide the decision.
08
Payroll Services
Multi-State ProcessingTax ComplianceBenefits IntegrationReporting

Payroll is among the most compliance-sensitive functions in any organization — carrying exposure across federal, state, and local tax jurisdictions, benefits administration, and employment law. Brant manages end-to-end payroll processing with the accuracy and regulatory precision your employees and business depend on. We handle multi-state registration, payroll tax filings, W-2 and 1099 preparation, and benefits deduction integration.

Core Deliverables
✓ Full-cycle payroll processing
✓ Multi-state employer registration & filings
✓ Federal, state & local payroll tax compliance
✓ W-2, W-3 & 1099 preparation & distribution
✓ Benefits deduction & garnishment processing
✓ Quarterly & annual payroll reporting
📌 This Service Is Right for You If
Your payroll function has become too complex or compliance-sensitive to manage internally — and a mistake carries regulatory and reputational consequences you cannot afford.
09
Bookkeeping Services
AR / AP ManagementBank ReconciliationMonth-End CloseFinancial Statements

Clean, accurate books are the foundation of every sound financial decision. Brant maintains meticulous bookkeeping records for businesses of all sizes — providing the financial visibility and discipline that enables confident, data-driven management. Our engagements encompass full-cycle AR and AP management, bank reconciliation, month-end close procedures, and timely financial statements.

Core Deliverables
✓ Full-cycle AR & AP management
✓ Bank & credit card reconciliation
✓ Monthly financial statement preparation
✓ Month-end close & adjusting entries
✓ GL maintenance & chart of accounts management
✓ Catch-up bookkeeping & historical cleanup
📌 This Service Is Right for You If
Your books are behind, inaccurate, or taking too much time away from running your business — and you need a trusted professional to establish the financial discipline your organization needs.
10
Cross-Functional Consulting
OperationsIT InfrastructureProcess EngineeringMarketing ROI

Enterprise value is created and destroyed across every function — not only in finance. Brant delivers cross-functional consulting that diagnoses inefficiencies, eliminates value leakage, and engineers the operational and technological infrastructure required for sustainable scale. Our engagements span IT infrastructure optimization, CRM and ERP implementation, marketing ROI analysis, workflow automation, and organizational redesign.

Core Deliverables
✓ Operational process audit & reengineering
✓ IT infrastructure & system integration
✓ CRM & ERP implementation support
✓ Marketing ROI & channel performance analysis
✓ Workflow automation & efficiency modeling
✓ Organizational design & talent structure review
📌 This Service Is Right for You If
Your business is generating revenue but struggling to scale efficiently — with fragmented systems, bottlenecks, or underperforming functions limiting profitability and growth capacity.
11
Construction Finance
Lien WaiversDraw ManagementRisk MitigationWIP Reporting

Construction projects carry unique and substantial financial risk — from complex draw schedules and AIA billing to retainage disputes, lien exposure, and subcontractor compliance. Brant provides specialized financial governance for multi-million dollar construction ventures, managing the origination, verification, and notarization of lien waivers, change orders, and COIs to secure absolute first-lien perfection. We govern the full financial lifecycle from groundbreaking through final closeout.

Core Deliverables
✓ Draw schedule management & AIA billing
✓ Lien waiver origination & notarization
✓ Change order & COI management
✓ WIP schedule & percentage-of-completion reporting
✓ Retainage tracking & subcontractor compliance
✓ Construction loan administration
📌 This Service Is Right for You If
You are managing a large-scale construction venture and require airtight financial controls and experienced oversight to safeguard cash flow and protect your position throughout the project lifecycle.
12
Legal Finance & Accounting
IOLTA ManagementTrust AccountingBar ComplianceThree-Way Reconciliation

The financial management of a law firm operates under a distinct and demanding regulatory framework. Brant provides specialized financial services for legal professionals — with particular expertise in IOLTA account management, trust accounting, and the rigorous three-way reconciliation requirements mandated by state bar associations. We ensure client funds are handled with absolute precision and that the firm’s records can withstand regulatory scrutiny.

Core Deliverables
✓ IOLTA account setup & ongoing management
✓ Client trust fund tracking & disbursement
✓ Three-way reconciliation (bank, ledger, client ledger)
✓ Bar association compliance reporting
✓ Firm operating account bookkeeping
✓ Attorney disbursement & billing reconciliation
📌 This Service Is Right for You If
Your firm manages client trust funds and requires a financial partner who understands the non-negotiable compliance obligations of legal accounting.

Ready to Elevate Your Financial Operations?

Brant Professional Services engages with organizations that demand institutional-grade expertise and a genuine long-term partner. Reach out to begin the conversation.

Recommended Reading
Book List

Curated titles recommended by Brant Professional Services. These books represent some of the most enduring thinking on business, leadership, finance, and strategy. Affiliate links coming soon.

⭐  Book of the Month
Extraordinary Guarantees
by Christopher W. L. Hart

A landmark guide demonstrating how bold, unconditional guarantees transform service quality, build deep customer trust, and create formidable competitive advantage. Hart reveals why the strongest guarantee a company can offer is also its most powerful management tool.

View on Amazon →
Complete Reading List

Recommended Library

A New Brand World
Scott Bedbury
Bedbury, architect behind Nike's 'Just Do It' and Starbucks' global rise, delivers an insider's blueprint for building iconic brands through emotional authenticity and unwavering consistency.
View on Amazon →
Atomic Habits
James Clear
The definitive playbook on behavior change. Small improvements compounded over time produce extraordinary results — a practical system for building high-performance habits across individuals and organizations.
View on Amazon →
Competing on Value
Hanan Mack
A strategic framework for businesses that want to win on substance rather than price — making the compelling case that delivering genuine, measurable value is the only durable path to market leadership.
View on Amazon →
Differentiate or Die
Jack Trout & Steve Rivkin
A sharp, authoritative argument that differentiation is the only sustainable competitive strategy. Trout and Rivkin dissect how brands survive in an era of overwhelming market noise.
View on Amazon →
Execution
Larry Bossidy & Ram Charan
One of the most important books on the discipline of getting things done. Strategy means nothing without the right people, processes, and culture to execute it.
View on Amazon →
First, Break All the Rules
Marcus Buckingham & Curt Coffman
Backed by Gallup's landmark study of 80,000 managers, this book reveals what the world's greatest managers do differently — and why conventional wisdom about managing people is consistently wrong.
View on Amazon →
Get Better or Get Beaten!
Robert Slater
A distillation of Jack Welch's leadership philosophy — 29 principles for competing, winning, and building organizations that continuously outperform. Timeless lessons from one of history's most effective executives.
View on Amazon →
Good to Great
Jim Collins
Collins' landmark research on why certain companies make the leap to sustained greatness while others remain merely good — a data-driven examination of the disciplines that separate elite organizations.
View on Amazon →
Inside the Magic Kingdom
Thomas K. Connellan
Seven customer service principles drawn from Disney's world-class operations, translated into practical lessons for any organization serious about delivering exceptional client experiences.
View on Amazon →
Leadership
Rudolph W. Giuliani
Firsthand lessons in crisis leadership and decisive decision-making drawn from real-world experience leading the nation's largest city through extraordinary challenges.
View on Amazon →
McDonald's: Behind the Arches
John F. Love
The definitive account of how McDonald's became a global empire — a masterclass in operational excellence, franchising strategy, and building scalable systems that deliver consistent quality worldwide.
View on Amazon →
Principle-Centered Leadership
Stephen R. Covey
Covey's framework for building organizations grounded in timeless principles — essential for leaders committed to cultures of trust, integrity, and sustained high performance.
View on Amazon →
Practice What You Preach
David H. Maister
Empirical research proving that employee satisfaction, management quality, and financial performance are deeply interconnected — and that firms living their stated values consistently outperform those that do not.
View on Amazon →
Raving Fans
Ken Blanchard & Sheldon Bowles
A business parable redefining the standard for customer service. The goal isn't satisfied customers — it's clients so delighted they become passionate advocates for your business.
View on Amazon →
The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
Stephen R. Covey
One of the most influential business books ever written. Covey's character-based framework for personal and organizational effectiveness has shaped the leadership of millions of executives worldwide.
View on Amazon →
The E-Myth Manager
Michael E. Gerber
Gerber diagnoses why most managers fail and presents a compelling case for systems-based management — showing how the right approach frees people to perform at their highest level.
View on Amazon →
The E-Myth Revisited
Michael E. Gerber
Essential reading for any business owner. Gerber exposes the fatal flaw in how most businesses are built and provides a proven framework for creating systems that produce predictable, scalable results.
View on Amazon →
The Fourth Turning
William Strauss & Neil Howe
A provocative generational theory revealing that history moves in predictable cycles — with profound implications for business strategy, investment, and institutional leadership.
View on Amazon →
The Myth of Excellence
Fred Crawford & Ryan Mathews
Research proving that the most successful businesses dominate on one or two dimensions that matter most to customers and remain competitive everywhere else — a counterintuitive but data-backed strategic framework.
View on Amazon →
The Power Principle
Blaine Lee
A penetrating distinction between coercive power and principle-centered influence — and why leaders who build trust through character consistently outperform those who rely on authority or pressure.
View on Amazon →
The Sovereign Individual
James Dale Davidson & Lord William Rees-Mogg
A visionary work predicting how technology would disrupt nation-states, taxation, and wealth creation. Its thesis on the shift of power from institutions to individuals has proven remarkably prescient.
View on Amazon →
The Wealthy Barber
David Chilton
Plain-language financial wisdom delivered through an accessible narrative. Practical principles on saving, investing, and wealth accumulation that have helped millions build lasting financial independence.
View on Amazon →
Think and Grow Rich
Napoleon Hill
The timeless classic that has shaped more successful executives than perhaps any other book. Hill's philosophy on purpose, persistence, and the mastermind principle remains foundational for achievement at the highest level.
View on Amazon →
Who Says Elephants Can't Dance?
Louis V. Gerstner, Jr.
The definitive account of IBM's dramatic turnaround — a masterclass in decisive leadership, cultural transformation, and the relentless execution required to rescue a great company from irrelevance.
View on Amazon →
Why We Buy
Paco Underhill
Groundbreaking research revealing the environmental and psychological forces that drive purchasing decisions — transforming how the world's leading brands, retailers, and service businesses are designed.
View on Amazon →

Privacy Policy

Effective Date: May 13, 2026  ·  Brant Professional Services Inc.  ·  New York, USA

1. Introduction

Brant Professional Services Inc. (“Brant,” “we,” “us,” or “our”) provides consulting, advisory, and financial intermediation services, including connecting businesses with lenders, capital providers, and institutional investors. This Privacy Policy governs your use of our website and services and describes how we collect, use, share, and protect your information. By accessing or using our Services, you agree to this Policy.

2. Information We Collect

A. Information You Provide

  • Name, email, phone number, and contact details
  • Business information, ownership details, and operational data
  • Financial, tax, and credit-related information submitted for consulting or financing purposes
  • Documents uploaded or transmitted through our website or intake processes

B. Automatically Collected Information

  • IP address, browser type, device identifiers, and operating system
  • Usage activity, page interaction logs, and session data
  • Cookies and similar tracking technologies

C. Information from Third Parties

  • Credit bureaus and financial institutions
  • Data providers, referral partners, and service providers
  • Public records and business databases

3. How We Use Your Information

We use information for legitimate business purposes, including: providing consulting, advisory, tax, and financial services; evaluating and facilitating financing opportunities; matching clients with lenders and capital providers; internal analytics and underwriting; business development and client communications; and legal and regulatory compliance.

4. Sharing of Information

We may share your information with: lenders, capital providers, and institutional investors in connection with financing services; affiliated entities; vendors and contractors acting on our behalf; professional advisors; and government or regulatory authorities as required by law.

5. Financial Information — GLBA Notice

Where applicable, we collect and process nonpublic personal financial information in connection with financial services engagements. We maintain safeguards designed to protect such information as required under the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act.

6. California Privacy Rights (CCPA/CPRA)

California residents may have the right to request access, deletion, or correction of personal information; opt out of certain data sharing; and limit use of sensitive personal information. Contact: info@brantprofessionalservices.com. We will not discriminate against you for exercising these rights.

7. New York Privacy Disclosures

We comply with applicable New York data protection and cybersecurity requirements, including the New York SHIELD Act and DFS Cybersecurity Regulation (23 NYCRR 500) where applicable. We implement risk-based security measures appropriate to the nature of our operations. In the event of a data breach involving private information of New York residents, we will provide notification as required by law.

8. Data Retention

We retain information for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this Policy and to comply with legal and regulatory obligations.

9. Data Security

We implement reasonable administrative, technical, and physical safeguards to protect information we collect. However, no system is completely secure. We will notify affected individuals and relevant authorities of any breach as required by applicable law.

10. Third-Party Services

Our Services may involve third-party lenders, partners, and websites. Brant is not responsible for their privacy practices or content. We encourage you to review their privacy policies.

11. Consent to Data Use & Communications

By using our Services or submitting information, you authorize our collection, verification, and use of your data; consent to sharing with partners and service providers; authorize review of financial and credit-related data where applicable; and consent to receive communications via phone, email, and SMS.

12. Limitation of Liability

To the fullest extent permitted by law, our Services are provided “as-is” and “as-available.” Brant disclaims liability for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages except where such liability cannot be disclaimed under applicable law.

13. Modifications

We may update this Privacy Policy at any time. Continued use of our Services following any modification constitutes your acceptance of the updated Policy.

14. Contact Information

Brant Professional Services Inc.
Email: info@brantprofessionalservices.com
Phone: (516) 655-6910
New York, USA

Terms of Use

Effective Date: May 13, 2026  ·  Brant Professional Services Inc.  ·  New York, USA

1. Acceptance of Terms

By accessing or using the services, website, or any resources provided by Brant Professional Services Inc. (“Brant”), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Use. If you do not agree, you may not access or use our Services. These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of New York.

2. Use of Services

You agree to use our Services solely for lawful business purposes, to provide accurate and complete information, to refrain from misrepresenting your identity or financial circumstances, and to comply with all applicable laws and regulations.

3. Nature of Services

Brant Professional Services Inc. provides consulting, advisory, tax and accounting, and financial intermediation services, including connecting businesses with lenders, capital providers, and institutional investors. These Terms govern general use of our Services and do not constitute an engagement agreement.

4. No Guarantee of Results

Brant does not guarantee any specific outcome, including: approval of any financing application; achievement of any specific business or financial outcome; or success of any advisory recommendation. All outcomes depend upon factors outside Brant’s control. Past results are not indicative of future outcomes.

5. Third-Party Relationships

Brant may connect you with lenders, capital providers, and other third parties. Brant is not responsible for decisions made by third parties, the terms offered by third parties, or the outcomes of any transaction involving a third party.

6. Brokerage Disclosure

In certain engagements, Brant may act as a broker or financial intermediary. You acknowledge that: Brant may receive compensation from third parties; Brant may present one or more financing options and is not obligated to present all available market options; and the existence of any compensation arrangement will not cause Brant to recommend services not in your interest as disclosed.

7. Data, Privacy & Communications

Your use of our Services is subject to our Privacy Policy, incorporated herein by reference. You consent to receive communications via phone, email, and SMS. You may opt out of marketing communications by contacting us at info@brantprofessionalservices.com.

8. Intellectual Property

All content, materials, tools, and intellectual property displayed through our website and Services are the property of Brant Professional Services Inc. or its licensors. You may not reproduce, distribute, or modify any content without prior written consent.

9. Limitation of Liability

To the fullest extent permitted by law: our Services are provided “as-is” without warranties of any kind; Brant disclaims liability for indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages; and our total liability shall not exceed fees paid by you to Brant in the twelve months preceding the claim.

10. Indemnification

You agree to indemnify and hold harmless Brant Professional Services Inc., its officers, employees, and affiliates from any claims, liabilities, or expenses arising from your use of our Services, your violation of these Terms, or your submission of inaccurate information.

11. Modifications

Brant reserves the right to modify these Terms at any time. Continued use of our Services following any modification constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.

12. Governing Law & Dispute Resolution

These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of New York. Any dispute shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in New York, New York.

13. Contact Information

Please Note: Each file must be 2MB or smaller. Accepted formats: PDF, JPG, PNG. For larger files, please email them directly to info@brantprofessionalservices.com along with your name, business name, and phone number. Additional documents may be requested during the underwriting process. All submitted documents are reviewed in confidence by Brant Professional Services.
